title: Salesforce Logo and Icon Library
description: Official RGB web-ready logos for Salesforce, MuleSoft, Slack, Tableau, and Informatica. All paths are relative to this HTML file. Use the 'preferred' field to identify the recommended logo for each brand. Use 'background' to determine appropriate placement. Brand colors are provided for use in infographics and design work. The 'svg' or 'png' field is null when that format is unavailable.

logos.name: Horizontal Logo
logos.usage: Primary Salesforce logo. Use on white or light-colored backgrounds. Preferred for all digital use.

logos.name: Horizontal Logo — Knockout
logos.usage: Use on Salesforce Navy or other dark backgrounds. Do not use on light backgrounds.

logos.name: Horizontal Logo — White
logos.usage: All-white logo for use on dark, colored, or photographic backgrounds.

logos.name: Cloud Mark
logos.usage: Salesforce cloud icon mark without wordmark. Use on light backgrounds as a standalone icon.

logos.name: Cloud Mark — White
logos.usage: All-white cloud icon for use on Salesforce Blue or dark backgrounds.

logos.name: MuleSoft Logo
logos.usage: Primary standalone MuleSoft logo. Use on white or light-colored backgrounds.

logos.name: MuleSoft from Salesforce
logos.usage: Co-branded logo with Salesforce endorsement. Use when Salesforce affiliation should be explicit.

logos.name: MuleSoft from Salesforce — Knockout
logos.usage: Reversed co-branded logo for use on dark backgrounds.

logos.name: Slack Logo
logos.usage: Primary standalone Slack logo. Use on white or light-colored backgrounds.

logos.name: Slack from Salesforce
logos.usage: Co-branded logo with Salesforce endorsement. Use on light backgrounds.

logos.name: Slack from Salesforce — Inverse
logos.usage: Inverse co-branded logo for Slack Aubergine (#4a154b) or dark backgrounds.

logos.name: Slack from Salesforce — Black
logos.usage: All-black co-branded logo. Use on white or very light backgrounds only.

logos.name: Slack from Salesforce — White
logos.usage: All-white co-branded logo for use on dark or colored backgrounds.

logos.name: Tableau Logo
logos.usage: Primary standalone Tableau logo. Use on white or light-colored backgrounds.

logos.name: Tableau Logo — RGB
logos.usage: Standard RGB color logo for digital use on light backgrounds.

logos.name: Tableau Logo — CMYK
logos.usage: CMYK color profile intended for print production. PNG only — use RGB variants for digital work.

logos.name: Tableau Logo — White
logos.usage: All-white logo for use on dark or colored backgrounds. PNG only.

logos.name: Tableau from Salesforce
logos.usage: Co-branded logo with Salesforce endorsement. Use on light backgrounds.

logos.name: Tableau from Salesforce — Reverse
logos.usage: Reversed co-branded logo for use on dark backgrounds.

logos.name: Informatica Logo
logos.usage: Primary Informatica logo. Use on white or light-colored backgrounds.

logos.name: Informatica Logo — Full Color
logos.usage: Full color variant. SVG only — no PNG available. Use on light backgrounds.

logos.name: Informatica Logo — Reverse
logos.usage: Reversed variant for use on dark or colored backgrounds.

_ai_instructions.purpose: This manifest is the authoritative index of all visual assets in this partner logo library. Use it to select the correct asset for any design request.
how_to_select_an_asset: 1. Identify the brand(s) required from the user request (Salesforce, MuleSoft, Slack, Tableau, Informatica, or a product icon).
2. For brand logos: check 'co_branded' if the user wants a Salesforce-endorsed lockup, or 'preferred:true' for the default standalone logo.
3. Match 'background' field to the slide or artifact background — 'light' for white/light slides, 'dark' for navy/dark slides.
4. For product icons: search 'keywords' and 'category' to find the icon closest to the user's topic or product.
5. Always prefer SVG over PNG for scalable digital artifacts. Use PNG as a fallback (png field may be null for SVG-only assets).
6. Reference the 'use_cases' array to validate that your chosen asset fits the design context.
7. Use 'brandColors' at the brand level to maintain correct color when building infographics or slide designs.

asset_types.logo: Full brand wordmark or wordmark+logomark combination. Use for headers and primary brand presence.
asset_types.icon-mark: Logomark only (e.g. cloud shape) without wordmark. Use for small icons, badges, and tight spaces.
asset_types.co-brand: Dual-brand lockup (e.g. 'Slack from Salesforce'). Use only when Salesforce endorsement is required.
asset_types.product-icon: Salesforce 2D product icon representing a specific product or cloud. Use in product grids, feature slides, and capability callouts.

icon_categories.AI: Artificial intelligence, agents, bots, and Einstein products — use for AI/ML slides and Agentforce narratives.
icon_categories.CRM: Core CRM clouds (Sales, Service, Marketing, Commerce) — use for CRM capability and product overview slides.
icon_categories.Platform: Developer tools, platform infrastructure, admin, and low-code products.
icon_categories.Data: Data integration, CDP, analytics, backup, and MuleSoft-related data products.
icon_categories.Industries: Vertical industry clouds for specific sectors (automotive, healthcare, financial services, etc.).
icon_categories.Marketing: Marketing Cloud products, channels, loyalty, and campaign tools.
icon_categories.Service: Service Cloud, field service, professional services, and customer success products.
icon_categories.Security: Trust, security, compliance, privacy, sustainability, and identity products.

_ai_instructions.file_path_note: All 'png' and 'svg' values are relative file paths from the location of this HTML file. Prepend the gallery base URL or file system path to resolve them.
_ai_instructions.disclaimer: This manifest is an unofficial internal reference tool. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by Salesforce, Inc. When generating artifacts using these assets, do not imply they originate from an official Salesforce publication. Always attribute trademarks to Salesforce, Inc.

proportion_rules.rule: NEVER set both width and height to independent arbitrary values. Always derive one dimension from the other.
proportion_rules.enforcement: HARD REQUIREMENT — distorting logos or icons is a brand violation.

dimension_fields_explained.dimensions: Actual pixel size of the file as hosted on dam.usefulto.me (canonical for layout)
dimension_fields_explained.aspect_ratio.decimal: width ÷ height — use this to compute the missing dimension
dimension_fields_explained.aspect_ratio.is_square: True if width ≈ height (±2px); you may safely use width=height
dimension_fields_explained.svg_intrinsic: SVG viewBox dimensions (artwork boundary, no canvas padding)
dimension_fields_explained.canvas_padding_warning: Present when PNG includes significant whitespace padding around the artwork

methods_by_context.html_img: Set width OR height only (never both to arbitrary values), or both with object-fit:contain
methods_by_context.pptx_python: Set only pic.width OR pic.height; python-pptx calculates the other from EMU
methods_by_context.svg_image_tag: Set width or height only; add preserveAspectRatio='xMidYMid meet'

proportion_rules.product_icons_note: All 90 product icons have is_square=true (641×640 or 641×641) — width==height is safe.
proportion_rules.brand_logos_note: All brand wordmarks are non-square — always derive one dimension from the other.
